definition_origin: AI-slop is the term (popularized 2024-2026) for visually-recognizable defaults of AI image generators that signal 'AI-made, low-effort, generic'. In illustration: gradient blob meshes, isometric voxel scenes, 3D blobby cute characters, melted-finger humans, gibberish text on signs. In mascots: chibi-Disney defaults, derivative Duolingo-Duo postures. Slop is identified by SAMENESS across thousands of unrelated apps. Counter-slop = intentional imperfection, hand-drawn texture, archetype-anchored personality, custom over stock.
core_purpose: At Norman's VISCERAL level, slop instantly triggers 'cheap / untrustworthy / generic' — the user pattern-matches to LinkedIn AI thumbnails and dismisses. At BEHAVIORAL level, slop fails to convey hierarchy because all elements have the same AI-default character. At REFLECTIVE level, shipping slop signals 'we did not care' and craters brand pride. Anti-slop discipline = competitive moat in 2026 because most apps will look identical via AI-default; intentional craft is therefore the cheapest differentiator a solo dev has.
when_needed: MANDATORY for any consumer-facing surface (marketing landing, app icon, onboarding, empty states, share-cards). Less critical for internal admin/B2B dashboards but still impacts trust. Most critical at first-impression surfaces where slop = bounce. Stock-illustration slop especially deadly on marketing landing pages where conversion depends on perceived legitimacy.
format_options: Slop-free format choices favor: hand-drawn PNG/WebP with intentional jitter > vector SVG with AI-perfection; bespoke single-artist Lottie > LottieFiles community downloads; custom 3D from Spline/Blender > AI-3D blobby renders; archetype-locked photography > stock Unsplash 'aesthetic'. Custom always > library. SVG is fine but the brushwork must be hand-feel; Procreate-exported PNG with paper texture often beats AI-vector.
file_size_performance: Hand-drawn texture means slightly bigger files (PNG with grain doesn't compress as well); accept 100-300KB for hero illustrations vs 30-60KB for flat vector. Use WebP/AVIF for modern compression (50-70% smaller than PNG at same quality). Lottie hand-drawn can be heavier (300-800KB); convert to dotLottie for 30-50% reduction.
free_tools: FREE ANTI-SLOP CRAFT TOOLS: Procreate-style alternatives — Krita (free, open-source desktop, full digital painting suite). Excalidraw (free, browser; intentionally hand-drawn-feel diagrams; the original anti-slop signature). tldraw (free, browser; hand-feel vector drawing). Photopea (free Photoshop alternative in browser; full PSD compat). Penpot (free open-source design tool, supports custom assets). Affinity Designer (one-time $70, not free but listed as anti-Adobe option). Inkscape (free SVG editor, manual vector). Krea AI (free tier, more controllable than Midjourney for archetype-locked output). Recraft (free 30 credits/day; archetype-style locking; SVG export but cleanup required). Whisk (Google Labs, free until April 2026 retirement). Reroll dice for AI prompts (Promptbase, prompthero free communities). Pinterest + Are.na for moodboards that AVOID slop signatures. SLOP-INDICATOR libraries to AVOID as primary source: unDraw default style (now slop), Storyset isometric (now slop), IRA Design gradient (very slop), Open Doodles (less slop because of Pablo Stanley's hand-feel but still recognizable), Humaaans (mix-match slop signature). Use these only as STARTING points and modify aggressively.
paid_tools: STARTER ($10-15/mo): Procreate $13 one-time on iPad — best $13 a solo dev can spend for anti-slop. Affinity Designer $70 one-time. PRO ($20-30/mo): Midjourney $30/mo (more controllable than ChatGPT image gen for archetype lock). Linear-style hand-illustrated icon kits ($30-100 one-time on Iconscout Premium). ENTERPRISE ($50+/mo): Hire a freelance illustrator on Working Not Working or Dribbble for hero illustrations ($200-500 one-time per piece) — the only true anti-slop is human craft. Spending $300 on 3 bespoke hero illustrations beats $50/mo on AI tools.
gemini_pro_workflow: Gemini Pro is BOTH the slop generator AND the slop detector. Use it for: (1) Slop audit: 'Audit these illustrations for AI-slop tells. Score 0-10 on: gradient meshes, melted hands, generic isometric, blobby 3D, default chibi, unDraw-default-style. List specific issues.' (2) Counter-prompts: 'Rewrite this Imagen prompt to avoid AI-slop: original = X. Add hand-drawn texture, intentional imperfection, archetype anchor for {ARCHETYPE}, reference artist {ARTIST_NAME}, paper grain, ink bleed, asymmetry.' (3) Reference-locked Imagen 4 generation in Gemini: provide 3 reference images (one of your bespoke style + 2 of slop counter-examples) and Imagen 4 with 'reference image lock' produces output closer to your style. (4) Whisk subject+style+scene blending — feed YOUR hand-drawn sample as the style anchor. (5) NotebookLM with all 12 archetype docs + slop catalog generates archetype-anchored anti-slop prompt library. (6) Use Gemini long context to maintain a slop-anti-tokens.md file ('this brand will never use: gradient mesh, isometric voxel, blobby 3D character, melted-finger human, gibberish-text-on-signs, generic chibi, default unDraw skin tones') referenced in every prompt.
workflow_steps: Step 1 — Audit current assets (1 hr): score every existing asset 0-10 on slop indicators; flag anything <5. Step 2 — Define archetype anti-tokens (30 min): 'this brand will NEVER use [list 5-7 specific slop signatures]'. Step 3 — Find 3-5 reference artists (1 hr): real human illustrators on Dribbble/Are.na whose style anchors your archetype; save 30 reference images. Step 4 — Build a custom-style sample (3-4 hr): in Procreate/Krita, create ONE original illustration that defines the brand style (paper texture, brush feel, color palette, line weight, character proportions). Step 5 — Generate AI candidates with style lock (2 hr): Imagen 4 with reference image + style guidance prompt → 30-50 candidates → keep 5 that match. Step 6 — Hand-edit AI output (1-2 hr per asset): import to Procreate/Krita; redraw 30%+ of the strokes; add intentional imperfection; fix any melted-hands; fix gibberish text; add brand color overlay. Step 7 — Vectorize what should be vector (30 min): trace the cleaned illustration in Inkscape if SVG needed. Step 8 — Optimize and export (15 min): SVGO for vector, Squoosh for raster, name with archetype prefix. Step 9 — Validate against slop catalog (10 min): show 3 strangers and ask 'does this look AI-made?' — if >1 says yes, redo step 6.
ai_prompt_templates: Anti-slop illustration: 'Hand-drawn ink illustration of {SUBJECT}, paper grain texture, intentional asymmetry, varied stroke weights, {ARCHETYPE_PALETTE} limited to 3 colors, reference artist {ARTIST_NAME}, NO gradient mesh, NO isometric, NO 3D, NO chibi, no glow effects, line-based, flat-color fill, single-figure composition, transparent background.' Anti-slop mascot: 'Bespoke 2D mascot character {ANIMAL_OR_OBJECT}, archetype = {ARCHETYPE}, designed by {REFERENCE_ARTIST}, custom proportions (NOT chibi default), distinctive silhouette readable at 32px, single accent color, hand-drawn outline with deliberate weight variation, paper texture, 3 expressions (neutral, joyful, concerned), front pose only, no Disney-Pixar, no Duolingo-Duo derivative.' Anti-slop 3D: 'Stylized 3D render of {SUBJECT}, low-poly geometric (not blobby), matte materials no chrome no glass, single accent color, flat lighting (not 3-point studio), reference: {ARTIST}, NO subsurface scattering, NO photorealistic skin, NO floating objects, geometric primitive language.' Slop-detector prompt: 'Analyze this image for AI-slop tells. List specifically: gradient meshes Y/N, isometric voxel Y/N, blobby 3D Y/N, melted hands Y/N, default lighting Y/N, gibberish text Y/N, generic chibi Y/N, derivative-of-Duo Y/N, unDraw-default Y/N. Score overall 0-10.'
claude_code_integration: Maintain a /apps/mobile/src/design/anti-slop.md catalog of slop tells the brand will never ship. Build a /scripts/audit-illustrations.ts that scans /assets/illustrations for size + dimensions outliers (slop-AI tends to all be 1024x1024 squares). Wire pre-commit hook that runs SVGO and warns on any path containing 'undraw' / 'storyset' / 'humaaans' / 'iradesign' in filenames. Have Claude Code generate type-safe asset imports so accidentally-imported stock illustrations get caught at PR review. Add a Storybook (or Ladle) page showing every illustration on a single grid for human slop-spot review.
emotional_design_integration: VISCERAL-level anti-slop = first-frame craft signal — user trusts brand instantly. BEHAVIORAL-level = bespoke illustrations actually serve their teaching role (an empty-state illustration that's a generic AI-blob teaches nothing; a hand-drawn empty inbox character with archetype-aligned expression teaches everything). REFLECTIVE-level = anti-slop is the cheapest pride-of-association moat in 2026. Per archetype: CAREGIVER anti-slop = warm rough hand-drawn over gradient-mesh perfection. MAGICIAN anti-slop = restrained editorial illustration over generic 3D-glow. JESTER anti-slop = bold custom mascot over derivative-Duo chibi. SAGE anti-slop = data-clarity diagrams over generic line-art-startup-people. Each archetype has slop traps and counter-tokens.
specific_examples: ANTI-SLOP exemplars: Excalidraw (https://excalidraw.com/) — entire product is the anti-slop aesthetic; intentionally rough hand-drawn diagrams that became iconic. tldraw (https://www.tldraw.com/) — hand-drawn-feel UI as core identity. Linear's micro-illustrations (linear.app) — bespoke Pablo Stanley work, never stock. Stripe's hero illustrations (https://stripe.com/) — commissioned editorial 3D from Justin Maller / Yi-Chen Lee, instantly recognizable. Notion's hand-drawn style (https://www.notion.so/) — wonky organic shapes that feel custom because they ARE custom. Mailchimp Cyber Lucy mascot — bespoke illustrated brand world by Jessica Hische / Kate Friend studio. Duolingo Duo himself is bespoke (the SLOP is competitors copying Duo's stance/proportions). Headspace's hand-drawn dot characters (designed by Headspace in-house). SLOP exemplars (avoid): generic Web3-startup landing pages with isometric voxel city + 3D blobby characters + gradient mesh hero. SaaS-app empty states using unmodified Storyset orange-and-purple isometric. AI-generated marketing posts on LinkedIn with melted-finger humans. References: [Pablo Stanley's open work](https://www.openpeeps.com/), [Are.na anti-slop reference channels](https://www.are.na/), [Ben Stafford on anti-AI-illustration](https://www.benstafford.io/).
anti_ai_slop_traps: EXHAUSTIVE SLOP CATALOG (2026 currency): ILLUSTRATION SLOP — (1) gradient mesh blob hero (every Web3 startup); (2) isometric voxel city/landscape (Storyset signature); (3) 3D blobby cute character with oversized head and tiny limbs; (4) generic startup-people-with-laptops-in-flat-vector (unDraw default); (5) abstract geometric shapes floating in space (Linear's competitor knock-off look); (6) glow-on-dark-background dashboard mockup illustration. 3D SLOP — (1) chrome/glass material on every object; (2) 3-point studio lighting on EVERY scene; (3) subsurface-scattering skin on AI-generated humans; (4) melted/six-fingered hands; (5) gibberish text on signs/billboards/clothing; (6) impossible reflections; (7) blender-default Cycles render look. MASCOT SLOP — (1) chibi-Disney proportions (oversized head, tiny body, big eyes); (2) Duolingo-Duo derivative stance (one-foot-forward arms-spread); (3) generic 'cute animal in flat vector' (default Midjourney v6 output); (4) glossy-3D-rendered mascot with no archetype anchor; (5) AI-mascot wearing unrelated 'cool' accessories (sunglasses, headphones, hoodie) for 'personality'. STOCK-LIB SLOP — (1) raw unDraw illustrations on landing page (now signal 'lazy startup'); (2) IRA Design gradient style (very recognizable); (3) Humaaans mix-match characters without recoloring; (4) generic Lottie 'rocket launching' or 'team handshake' from LottieFiles community top-10. COUNTERS: (1) hand-drawn imperfection always; (2) brush in real Procreate/Krita on top of AI output; (3) one-color or very-limited palette; (4) intentional asymmetry; (5) archetype-anchor rather than 'generic cute'; (6) reference real artists not AI defaults; (7) commission a $200 hero illustration from Dribbble freelancer for landing page; (8) modify EVERY stock library asset before shipping (recolor, restyle, recompose).
common_pitfalls: (1) Solo dev syndrome: shipping unmodified unDraw because 'I'm not a designer' — this is now perceived as worse than no illustration. (2) Mixing 3 illustration styles on same screen (unDraw + Storyset + AI-generated). (3) Relying on AI defaults that are hardcoded into Midjourney's training (chibi, isometric). (4) Over-styling: adding noise, chromatic aberration, and grain ON TOP of AI-slop doesn't make it not slop. (5) Skipping the archetype lock — slop is what AI generates when you don't constrain it. (6) Ignoring legacy slop in marketing site while polishing in-app (first impression IS the marketing site). (7) Failing to budget for ONE bespoke piece — even one custom commissioned hero illustration shifts the entire brand perception.
accessibility_notes: Hand-drawn illustrations need higher contrast scrutiny — soft pastels often fail WCAG. Always provide alt text describing the illustration's intent ('illustration of a person hugging a heart, conveying care'). Don't communicate state via illustration alone (empty-state should also have text). Provide reduce-motion variants for any Lottie. Bespoke 3D often has heavier file sizes — provide low-bandwidth fallback PNG.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: bespoke illustrations work harder due to small canvas; one custom piece per surface beats five generic ones; sizes 1x/2x/3x for retina with WebP. Web: more illustration real-estate (hero, feature sections, testimonials); slop is more visible because users can compare across desktop tabs. Marketing-site landing slop = highest bounce risk.
sources: [Muzli: Best Free Illustration Libraries 2026](https://muz.li/blog/best-free-illustration-libraries-for-designers-2026/) — explicitly notes consistency over mixing libraries. [Muzli: Vibe Design in 2026](https://muz.li/blog/vibe-design-in-2026-what-ai-generated-ui-means-for-your-work/) — AI-design slop discussion. [unDraw](https://undraw.co/illustrations) — popular free library now widely identified as slop signature. [Humaaans by Pablo Stanley](https://www.humaaans.com/) — slop-prone but Stanley's own work is anti-slop reference. [Open Peeps](https://www.openpeeps.com/) — Pablo Stanley hand-drawn anti-slop alternative. [Excalidraw](https://excalidraw.com/) — anti-slop exemplar product. [Ben Stafford on anti-AI-art aesthetic](https://www.benstafford.io/) — practitioner perspective. [Are.na anti-slop reference channels](https://www.are.na/explore) — curated counter-aesthetic. [Recraft Review 2026](https://www.svggenie.com/blog/recraft-ai-review-2026) — notes SVG cleanup needed.
